steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the choice that emphasizes a similarity between the two artworks. The answer must state something true of both ArcelorMittal Orbit and Čumil the Sewer Worker — not just one, and not a difference.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'ArcelorMittal Orbit and Čumil the Sewer Worker are both on display at public locations.': Orbit is in a public park in London; Čumil is on a public street in Bratislava. Both are in public locations — this is a genuine similarity stated for both works. ✓
  • Choice B — 'ArcelorMittal Orbit, but not Čumil the Sewer Worker, can be found in London, England.': This highlights a difference, not a similarity, and the phrase "but not" makes that explicit. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Both ArcelorMittal Orbit and Čumil the Sewer Worker were created by Sir Anish Kapoor and Cecil Balmond.': The notes say Kapoor and Balmond created Orbit only; Čumil was created by Viktor Hulík. This is factually false. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Čumil the Sewer Worker, a bronze sculpture of a man emerging from under a manhole cover, is on display on a public street.': This describes only Čumil — it says nothing about Orbit, so it establishes no similarity between the two. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
